1. What; 2. How (noun) 1. snack (dim sum); 2. hour; (verb) 1. examine; 2. check; (verb) (of head) to nod

to give sb a hard time

1. allow; 2. let; (proposition) 1. to, 2. by or being in a passive voice
classifier for people or objects in general or an action
indicates approximation when placed before numbers and after a verb
adds emphasis when added before a complement used with a verb
describes someone who is nosy or likes to gossip

classifier for groups of people and scheduled transport vehicles
had better ...; it would be best to ...
to use; to make use of; to employ
to need; to have to (usu. used in the negative or in questions)
not equal to; not as good as; inferior to
used as the and in a mixed fraction e.g. 1- ½

to sell in large quantities and
